Finacle Digital Engagement Hub (DEH) Senior Analyst/Development lead to lead design, development, and delivery of digital banking integrations and APIs. Design scalable and reusable REST/SOAP APIs, integration flows, and DEH components.
Perform impact analysis for new requirements, Customer Migration, Finacle upgrades, and patches.
Development & Customization

Hands-on development using:

Java / J2EE
Spring / Spring Boot
RESTful services & JSON/XML
Quality, Performance & Security

Conduct design reviews and ensure high-quality deliverables.
Team & Stakeholder Management

Mentor junior developers and analysts.
Coordinate with Core backend teams, channel teams, infrastructure, and vendors.
Participate in design reviews, estimations, and release planning.

Strong hands-on experience with Finacle Digital Engagement Hub (DEH).

Strong Java/J2EE development experience.

REST / SOAP Web Services
JSON / XML

Experience with Finacle DEH implementation and upgrades.
Knowledge of security standards

Company description:



Deloitte Services Pty Ltd



Job description:




Senior Data Engineer | Data & Analytics | Sydney, AUS

Work on cutting-edge enterprise data solutions using Databricks, Azure Data Factory, DBT, and Python.

Collaborate with a high-performing, agile team to deliver impactful insights across Deloitte.

Continuous professional growth through mentoring, training, and exposure to emerging data engineering technologies.

Deloitte Technology is seeking a skilled Senior Data Engineer to join our Enterprise Data Warehouse (EDW) team in Sydney. This is a permanent role where you'll play a key part in designing, implementing, and optimising scalable data pipelines and workflows that power Deloitte's data-driven initiatives. You'll have the opportunity to work closely with EDW pod leads and the Senior Data Engineering Manager, contributing to high-impact projects across the business.

What will your typical day look like?

Day to day you will:

Develop and maintain scalable data pipelines using Databricks and Azure Data Factory.

Transform raw data into actionable insights using Data Build Tools (DBT) and Python.

Build and maintain data integrations across systems using APIs.

Monitor and optimise workflows for performance and cost-efficiency.

Lead project delivery pods, mentor junior team members, and conduct code reviews.

Collaborate with data modelers, analysts, DBAs, DevOps, and infrastructure teams to deliver high-quality solutions.

Document processes, workflows, and system architecture to maintain clarity and knowledge sharing.

About the team

Deloitte Technology's EDW team powers enterprise-wide data initiatives, enabling business users to make informed, data-driven decisions. You'll join a collaborative and high-performing team working in an agile environment, tackling complex data challenges and continuously improving the EDW platform.

Enough about us, let's talk about you

You may have all or some of the following skills / experiences:

5+ years of experience in data engineering, with expertise in Databricks, SQL, Python, Azure Data Factory, and DBT.

Experience designing, building, and optimising data pipelines and workflows.

Hands-on experience with API-based data integrations and version control systems (Azure DevOps, GitHub).

Strong understanding of data architecture patterns and cloud data storage solutions.

Excellent problem-solving skills, attention to detail, and adaptability.

Collaborative mindset with strong communication and stakeholder management skills.

Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field. Advanced degrees or relevant certifications in Azure or Databricks are a plus.

Desirable: Knowledge of Azure Fabric and emerging data engineering technologies.

Why Deloitte?

At Deloitte, we focus our energy on interesting and impactful work. We're always learning, innovating, and setting the standard; making a positive difference to our clients and society. We put coaching at the heart of what we do, helping our people grow their careers in any direction - up, into new areas, or across the globe.

We embrace diversity, equity, and inclusion. We have a diverse collection of people from different backgrounds, experiences, abilities, and thinking styles. What binds us together is a shared commitment to value everyone's perspective and cultivate inclusion, making our work environment a space where everyone belongs.

We support flexibility and choice. We encourage you to find the right balance between connecting in person with your clients and teams and meeting your own personal needs.

We help you live and work well. To support your personal and professional life, we offer a range of perks and benefits, including retail discounts, wellbeing leave, paid volunteering days, twelve flexible working options, market-leading parental leave, and a return-to-work support package.

Next Steps

Sound like the sort of role for you? Apply now, we'd love to hear from you!

Roles: Data Scientist and Data engineer
Location: Australia Wide
Baseline Security Clearance req

We are seeking an experienced Senior Data Scientist/Lead data analyst to lead and deliver advanced analytical capability for a national‑scale tertiary education data asset that underpins system performance reporting, evaluation and modelling.

This role requiring deep expertise in administrative data engineering, econometrics and governance of large, sensitive data sets. The position plays a critical role in sustaining complex data pipelines, applying advanced analytical methods, and delivering trusted, evidence‑based insights to inform policy and system‑level decision‑making.

You will provide technical leadership and strategic oversight across the full data lifecycle-from ingestion and integration through to analysis, modelling and publication of insights.

You will operate with a high level of autonomy, advising senior stakeholders, guiding analytical standards, and mentoring analysts and data scientists while delivering high‑impact outputs under complex policy and delivery constraints.

Key Responsibilities
Lead the design, build and maintenance of robust administrative data pipelines supporting national system performance analysis
Provide expert oversight of data ingestion, transformation and integration from multiple complex administrative sources
Apply and guide the use of advanced econometric, causal inference and statistical methods to measure post‑school education outcomes and support evaluation and modelling
Establish and enforce data quality, validation, documentation and reproducibility standards across analytical products
Deliver high‑quality analytical outputs, including system performance measures, modelling insights and executive‑ready briefings
Ensure confidentiality, privacy, security and governance requirements are fully embedded in analytical workflows
Provide technical leadership and mentoring to analysts and data scientists, uplifting capability across the team
Essential Capabilities

To be successful you will demonstrate:

Advanced coding expertise across multiple programming languages (e.g. Python, R, SQL), including production‑quality analytical workflows
Strong leadership in database management and data architecture, including large‑scale administrative datasets, performance optimisation and data integrity
Proven experience designing, governing and sustaining complex administrative data pipelines in policy‑intensive or service‑delivery environments
Deep experience applying advanced econometric and statistical techniques, including causal inference methods, to support evaluation, modelling and strategic analysis
Ability to translate complex analytical findings into clear, decision‑ready advice for senior stakeholders

Job Title: Senior Data Migration and BI Specialist
Ad Text:
** AUstralia PR / Citizens are encouraged to apply**



Your new company
A prominent state government department.


Your new role
As a Senior Data Migration & Business Intelligence Specialist, you will support our migration from a legacy on-premises Case Management System to a SaaS solution. This role will be instrumental in ensuring data integrity, building robust reporting solutions, and establishing best practices for business intelligence implementation that can be adopted by the internal team.


In this role you will,

Lead and execute data migration activities (from legacy to SaaS based Case Management System) including:
- Data extraction from legacy systems
- Develop Source to Template mapping documentation
- Data transformation and cleansing (including transformation logic to vendor provided JSON templates)
- File migration
- Development and execution of ETL scripts
- Testing success of deployed ETL scripts
- Working with structured and unstructured data
Design and implement Power BI dashboards and SSRS reports including rebuilding reports from migrated data
Develop and maintain dimensional data models including STAR schema, fact and dimension tables
Assist in designing data marts and data warehouses
Establish and document best practices for BI solution development and deployment

What you'll need to succeed

Proven experience delivering BI and data migration outcomes in partnership with a SaaS vendor, including working within vendor-defined data templates and constraints (e.g. JSON-based templates) and managing dependencies and deliverables.
Demonstrated experience designing and delivering BI solutions involving highly sensitive and confidential data, with strong awareness of privacy, compliance, access controls, and secure data handling throughout migration and reporting.
Strong experience performing data reconciliation, validation, and exception management across legacy-to-SaaS migrations, ensuring data integrity between source systems, vendor platforms, and reporting layers.
Proven ability to produce and maintain data migration and BI documentation, including source-to-target mappings, transformation rules, data dictionaries, reporting definitions, and dimensional model (STAR schema) documentation.
Demonstrated experience acting as a data migration or BI delivery lead, coordinating technical activities, engaging vendors and stakeholders, and supporting successful handover to internal teams.
Able to provide specific examples of recent BI or data migration work, including scope, tooling used, and outcomes delivered (particularly involving Power BI, Fabric, and data migration activities).
Experience with Case Management Systems (CMS).


What you'll get in return

Working in a state government organisation where they develop and grow careers.

A long-term opportunity offering excellent salary with flexible work arrangements


What you need to do now
If you're interested in this role or know of someone that may be, please click 'Apply Now' or forward an updated CV to Gopalakrishnan.subramanian@hays.com.au

Schneider Electric is leading the digital transformation of energy management and automation. Our technologies enable the world to use energy in a safe, efficient and sustainable manner. We strive to promote a global economy that is both ecologically viable and highly productive.  

 

The Opportunity:

As the world leader in energy procurement & compliance services with over 35 years' experience of helping clients reduce their energy spend & improving energy efficiency.  Our Melbourne CBD-based Energy and Sustainability Services division is seeking an Energy Procurement Senior Analyst to work on the Sourcing team that serves Australia and New Zealand sites to manage the many facets of the procurement process to meet client goals and contribute to the continued energy transition. Service coverage will also include Japan and Singapore, working with regional teams to orchestrate Australia energy market service capabilities.

 

Day to Day Responsibility:

As the Senior Analyst you will play an integral part in a well establish team of professionals who pride themselves on their positive impact and contribution to an inclusive work environment. You will be responsible for running RFP processes for electricity and gas supply contracts, network analysis, supporting budgets, understanding markets, training others, documentation processes and driving transformation. You will be confident to present to clients, online and in person (when appropriate). As the subject matter expert, you will mentor your team members and serve as a support for them.

 

Key responsibilities for this role include, but are not limited to;

 

Engage with Client Managers and other internal teams to deliver an exceptional level of service to our clients.
Engage with clients to discuss tender results and recommendations
Identify and communicate opportunities to reduce clients’ energy spend through tendering, tariff analyses, meter amalgamation, advanced procurement strategies, etc.
Manage pre-tender activities through collecting interval data, invoices, and contracts.
Manage the tendering, analysis and subsequent contract award and placement processes relating to complex energy supply contracts.
Negotiate and utilise quantitative and qualitative techniques to deliver optimal contract energy supply terms and prices for our clients through regular interaction with energy retailers.
Analyse network tariffs and recommend changes to reduce clients’ network costs.
Own and develop/enhance business processes to the highest standards of efficiency, control and accuracy.
Innovate processes or services to digitise & automate processes, improve effectiveness and efficiency, and improve the client experience.
Develop & deliver accurate & timely reports, responding to client queries as required; proactively communicate with internal and external clients.
Utilise and contribute to development of internal Schneider Electric procurement platforms, ensuring development suits local needs and establishing/implementing procedures which utilise the proprietary systems.

 

 

Short listing for these opportunities will require the following skills, attribute and qualifications;

 

Applicants with tertiary qualification in Economics, Mathematics, Computer Science, or Accounting/Finance are preferred.
3-5 years energy procurement or energy analytics experience preferable
Handling large data sets
Procurement experience within the energy industry either at a supplier or energy consultancy will be highly regarded.
Advanced MS-Excel skills are essential with the ability to identify, analyse, and comprehend information from numerous sources, and communicate pertinent information to the appropriate parties.
Demonstrable analytical ability with a sharp eye for numerical detail is essential, as is the ability to understand and discuss potential financial outcomes.
Proven ability to remain focused in a complex and fluid environment whilst delivering tasks accurately and on time.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills, customer-focused and good stakeholder management skills.
Adaptable, resilient and focused, capable of taking ownership and delivering desirable outcomes within a complex and changing environment whilst remaining an integral part of a close-knit and friendly team.

 

Additional benefits working in this role:

Flexible work options and Global Family Leave

Employee Share Program participation, including discounts and company contributions
Option to purchase additional annual leave
Discounts at major retailers such as Clipsal, JB Hi‑Fi, and Hoyts through our employee rewards program
Continuous training on Schneider products
Exposure to a global leader in sustainability and automation
Access to global support and resources
A well‑balanced, inclusive team environment

We’re a great place to work—and we’re continually striving to be the best. Join us and see how.

If you are looking for an opportunity to continue growing your career within our Leading Global Organisation then we want to hear from you. 

All applicants must have working rights in Australia at the time of application and will be required to provide documented evidence if selected for interview.

About the Team


This team is responsible for accelerating sustainable expansion across the Restaurant Marketplace - unlocking new value for customers, merchants, and the platform. We operate at the intersection of strategy, execution, and experimentation. Our remit spans city-level performance, merchant-led growth opportunities, and scalable initiatives that drive demand, frequency, and long-term marketplace strength.

Within Growth, the Local Markets team focuses on winning city by city - ensuring that our strategies resonate deeply with local communities and outperform competitors on the ground. Together, we combine granular local insight with scalable growth thinking.

About the Role


We are hiring a Senior Manager, Growth & Market Performance to drive growth initiatives across the Restaurant Marketplace and to lead our Local Markets team across Australia and New Zealand.

This role has two core pillars; Marketplace Growth Acceleration - Identifying and executing new growth opportunities across merchants and customer value propositions to unlock incremental scale. Local Market Strategy & Execution - Defining and winning city-level strategies by operating at the lowest level of detail.

You will identify winning strategies, design bold experiments to validate new ideas, and make investment decisions that strengthen our competitive position—both locally and at scale.

This is a highly cross-functional leadership role, partnering with Marketing, Operations, Sales, Product, Analytics, and Finance to uncover and execute the growth formula that resonates with customers and merchants.

You will operate as a growth owner - comfortable shaping strategy at a high level while diving deep into data and execution mechanics. You thrive in ambiguity and can set ambitious goals that people thought are unachievable.

This will be a hybrid role with 3 days a week in Office (Melbourne / Sydney) and 2 days remote.

You’re excited about this opportunity because you will…

Define and execute city-level growth strategies to win in priority markets, identifying structural opportunities across merchant offerings, selection expansion, and customer experience
Translate data, first-principles thinking, and marketplace insights into clear, high-impact growth roadmaps and prioritised initiatives
Deep dive into data to uncover actionable insights, assess strategic and financial impact, and partner with Analytics and Finance to evaluate ROI and optimise investments
Design, launch, and scale experiments across pricing, merchandising, and merchant/customer value propositions—rapidly testing, learning, and iterating
Lead cross-functional collaboration across Marketing, Operations, Sales, Product, and Finance, influencing stakeholders and representing the voice of the customer and local market
We’re excited about you because you have…

10+ years of experience in strategy, operations, consulting, growth, product, marketplace, or general management roles
Proven track record of owning and scaling growth initiatives in complex environments
Strong marketplace intuition, ideally within multi-sided or regulated environments
Deep comfort operating at the lowest level of detail - advanced analytics, experimentation, dashboards
Exceptional cross-functional leadership skills, with the ability to influence without authority
Strong business judgment and capital allocation instincts
Grit, resilience, and a bias to action in ambiguous, fast-moving environments

Senior Technical Business Analyst | Infrastructure Network Modernisation | 12-month contract | Brisbane

Your new company

This large Brisbane based government organisation is undertaking a significant Infrastructure and Security Modernisation program to strengthen and future‑proof its digital foundations. The program spans network modernisation, cybersecurity uplift, cloud and on-prem infrastructure platforms, monitoring and observability, resilience and disaster recovery, supporting critical services that underpin one of the largest and most complex operational environments in the state.


Your new role


As a Senior Technical Business Analyst, you will play a key role within a complex, enterprise infrastructure and security modernisation program. Working closely with the Senior Project Manager, Infrastructure & Security Domain Architect, solution architects, engineers and operational teams, you will provide specialist business analysis capability across a range of technically complex initiatives.


You will lead the elicitation, analysis and documentation of business, technical and operational requirements for network and security initiatives, translating complex subject matter into clear, actionable artefacts that support delivery. A key part of the role involves developing and contributing to large business cases and investment proposals, including options analysis, benefits, costs, risks and operational impacts.



What you'll need to succeed


Demonstrated experience as a Senior or Technical Business Analyst delivering complex ICT initiatives in large environments.
Proven experience working on infrastructure and/or cybersecurity projects, ideally within large, complex or public sector organisations.
Strong understanding of digital infrastructure domains, including:
Networks and connectivity
Cybersecurity and identity platforms
Cloud and on-premises infrastructure
Monitoring, logging and observability tools
Disaster recovery and resilience
Extensive experience eliciting, documenting and managing functional and non-functional requirements for technically complex solutions.

What you'll get in return


12‑month contract with strong likelihood of extension
Hybrid working model
Brisbane CBD location (Brisbane Square)
Opportunity to work on a highly visible infrastructure and security transformation program within a large-scale government environment

What you need to do now

About us – Australian Reinsurance Pool Corporation (ARPC)

ARPC’s purpose as a public financial corporation is to make a meaningful difference to Australian communities by providing sustainable and effective reinsurance for terrorism and cyclone events.

Our vision is to support insurers to deliver affordable terrorism and cyclone insurance in Australia. At ARPC, our values of Integrity, Respect, Service, and Wellbeing shape how we work and deliver outcomes.

Opportunity to be involved

The Manager Actuarial Reserving and Capital leads ARPC’s actuarial reserving and capital management function and is a key advisor to the Head of Actuarial.

Reporting to the Head of Actuarial, the role is responsible for delivering ARPC’s actuarial valuation of outstanding claims liabilities and premium liabilities, supporting capital projections, and providing financial risk insights that inform executive and Board decision-making.

This role plays a critical part in maintaining ARPC’s financial resilience and contributes to key organisational outputs including the Annual Valuation Report and Financial Outlook Report.

 

Role responsibilities

Lead the delivery of ARPC’s annual actuarial valuation and ongoing reserve estimates, ensuring compliance with professional standards and organisational policies.

Maintain and enhance ARPC’s capital projection model, providing insights that support capital management and the Financial Resilience Framework.

Model cashflow and liquidity requirements in collaboration with Finance to support effective financial management.

Contribute actuarial inputs into the Financial Outlook Report and annual budgeting processes, including projections of premium and claims costs.

Provide high-quality technical advice and briefing material to the Head of Actuarial, CFO and Board on reserving and capital matters.

Own and maintain actuarial model governance frameworks, including documentation, validation, change control and model risk oversight.

Lead engagement with external stakeholders including the Cyclone Pool Reviewing Actuary, external actuaries and auditors.

Embed sound risk management practices, proactively identifying risks and implementing mitigating actions.

Lead and develop team capability, including management of the Reserving and Capital Analyst, and contribute to broader actuarial and corporate initiatives.

Candidate Attributes

Qualifications

Fellowship of the Institute of Actuaries of Australia — Mandatory

Tertiary qualification in Actuarial Studies, Economics, Finance, Statistics or Commerce — Mandatory

Experience

Experience in the insurance, reinsurance or financial services sector— Mandatory.

Experience delivering actuarial valuations, capital modelling or financial projections — Highly Desirable

Experience engaging with senior stakeholders and translating complex data into clear insights— Highly Desirable

Exposure to regulated or government environments — Desirable

 

Technical capability

Strong analytical capability with the ability to derive clear insights from complex data

Experience with programming languages (e.g. SQL, SAS, R, Python) and data visualisation tools (e.g. Power BI)

Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to influence senior stakeholders

Demonstrated ability to build productive working relationships across internal and external stakeholders

High attention to detail and ability to operate as a subject matter expert

 

Note

The role is based in Sydney CBD, and you must be an Australian citizen and be able to obtain the Australian Government's baseline security clearance successfully.

Please apply for this position with a resume and covering letter, your application will be reviewed by a member of the HR team and suitable candidates will be invited to an interview for the role.

Salary: Above market salary, hybrid work culture
Ad Text:
About the company

My client is a leading global multinational bank and financial services company. It is headquartered and listed in the Australian stock exchange and employs nearly 15,000 staff in over 20 countries, is the world's largest asset manager in infrastructure and Australia's top ranked mergers and acquisitions adviser with almost half a trillion dollars in assets under management.

About the role

Because of their immense growth in their Data Architecture portfolio, an exciting opportunity has come up in one of their offices in Sydney. You will be joining their elite team and playing a lead role in providing the specialist skills needed to drive the maturity of their next generation data platform on hybrid cloud (AWS and GCP). They are urgently looking for data modelers to provide critical support to their Data Enablement portfolio and provide a robust data modelling structure in their growing team. Experience working in a cloud (AWS and GCP) is essential for this role.

Skill Sets

You need to have the following skill sets to excel in this role:

Extensive experience working in Financial services organizations like banks or insurances.
5+ years of data modelling experience in enterprise environments (working in businesses using cloud either GCP/AWS would be beneficial)
In-depth knowledge of data warehousing and experience working with data engineers is essential for this role.
Experience in building data products on data mesh concepts (highly preferred)
Understanding of data modeling principles across conceptual, logical and physical data models.
Experience in different data modeling techniques and design patterns like 3nf, Data Vault, kimball etc
Experience in using various data modelling tools: Erwin, ER/Studio, Sparx etc
Experience leading discussions and presentations. Experience in driving decisions across groups of stakeholders.

Responsibilities

Liaise closely with the Information Architect team to ensure delivery is in accordance with the agreed architecture, High Level Solution Designs and Information Strategy.
Liaise with the Operations teams very early to ensure aspects of quality, performance and stability are considered before a solution is designed.
Deliver high quality design in alignment with information and Analytics & BI architecture.
Provide technical design services which may include dimensional modelling, staging areas, Business Access Layers, semantic layer design and ETL design.
Work closely with Data Engineers and other stakeholders in the data domain to produce a robust data modeling solutions.

It is an urgent position, and my client is interviewing candidates at the moment. It is an exceptional opportunity for someone who has excellent Data modeling experience and has experience working in Australian Banks and financial services organisations. Only Australian Permanent Residents and Citizens required for this role.

For more information on the role, please either apply or contact Nathan Sabherwal on 0430880196. As the Lead Pega System Analyst, you will spearhead the design and delivery of high-tier business applications on the Pega platform. Your mission is to translate sophisticated operational needs into streamlined digital workflows, ensuring the agency's important systems remain robust and efficient.

Key Responsibilities

Ad Text:
About the Role
We are seeking an experienced Program Manager to lead a large-scale Digital Business Transformation across the student and academic lifecycle.

This role is accountable for delivering the digital aspects of a multi-domain transformation program spanning CRM (student recruitment and engagement), curriculum and course management, timetabling, learning management systems, student administration and student engagement platforms.

The role spans the full lifecycle — from architecture alignment and business process redesign through to supplier sourcing, delivery execution, data migration and operational handover. A key focus is ensuring platforms and processes are simplified, standardised and aligned to a future-state operating model that improves student experience and academic delivery.

Operating at an enterprise level, you will lead a complex, multi-stream program across academic and professional domains, ensuring outcomes are delivered in a coordinated, integrated and operationally sustainable manner.

This is not a standalone system implementation role. It is an enterprise-wide transformation role spanning digital platforms, processes, data and operating model change.

Key Responsibilities

Lead end-to-end delivery of the Digital Business Transformation program across student and academic systems
Establish and manage integrated program plans, including dependencies, milestones, financials and benefits realisation across multiple domains
Lead alignment of platform implementation with redesigned student and academic lifecycle processes (recruitment, admissions, curriculum, timetabling, learning delivery and student engagement)
Translate enterprise and domain architectures into executable delivery roadmaps, phased releases and transition states
Lead multi-stream, multi-vendor delivery across platforms including CRM, curriculum systems, LMS, timetabling and student systems
Support sourcing activities, including supplier engagement, onboarding and mobilisation
Oversee data migration, data quality and reporting across student and academic platforms
Drive disciplined program governance, including risk, issue, dependency and quality management
Lead service transition and operational readiness, ensuring platforms are embedded and supported within business and IT operations
Establish and track measurable benefits including student experience improvements, operational efficiency and data quality uplift

About You

To be successful in this role, you will have:
Demonstrated experience leading large-scale digital business transformation programs, ideally in higher education or similarly complex environments
Proven delivery of enterprise platforms supporting the student or customer lifecycle (e.g. CRM, LMS, curriculum, timetabling or core operational systems)
Experience delivering end-to-end transformation, including business process redesign, platform implementation, data migration and operational handover
Experience operating in multi-vendor environments, including supplier engagement and delivery coordination
Strong understanding of integration, data and process dependencies across complex platform ecosystems
Experience leading organisational change and business adoption alongside technology delivery
Ability to operate at a senior level, influencing academic leadership, business stakeholders and executive teams

Qualifications

Postgraduate qualification in a relevant discipline and extensive relevant experience; or an equivalent combination of relevant experience, and/or education/training.
MSP, PgMP or PMP certification (or equivalent program delivery experience).

Job Title: Head of Central Planning & Inventory (Anko Global)
Ad Text:
Brighter Futures Start Here

We are an inclusive team who power two of the most iconic and loved brands, Kmart and Target. We have more than 50,000 passionate individuals across our stores, distribution centres, and offices around the globe.

With our brands touching the lives of almost two million customers every week, you'll feel the impact of the work you'll do every day. As a leader in the retail market, we're optimistic about our future and yours. We're changing at a rapid rate, giving you challenging work that you'll be proud o

Purpose of the role


The Head of Central Planning & Inventory plays a critical leadership role in shaping how Anko Global plans, forecasts, and manages inventory across fast‑growing international markets. This role is accountable for building and leading scalable planning systems, processes, and operating frameworks that enable confident trading decisions, optimise stock outcomes, and support sustainable growth as the business expands globally.

Working at the intersection of merchandise planning, inventory, category, technology, and data, this role ensures Anko Global has robust, data‑driven planning capabilities that drive availability, margin optimisation, and inventory productivity.

What you will deliver:

In this role, you will be expected to:

Lead enterprise-wide merchandise planning capability
Own the design, integration, and ongoing evolution of central planning and inventory systems, ensuring they support end‑to‑end merchandise lifecycle planning, forecasting, allocation, replenishment, and pricing.
Build scalable planning frameworks and operating models
Establish consistent planning rhythms, governance, and decision frameworks across seasonal, in‑season, and event‑based cycles, enabling accuracy and discipline across multiple markets and categories.
Enable strong commercial outcomes
Partner closely with Merchandise, Category, Supply Chain, Finance, and Country teams to translate trading strategies into system‑enabled plans that drive sales, margin, forecast accuracy, and healthy stock positions.
Lead system transformation and change delivery
Drive cross‑functional planning and inventory transformation initiatives, including prioritisation of system enhancements, user adoption, training, and benefits realisation.
Strengthen data integrity and insight
Ensure the quality and integrity of planning master data, forecasting inputs, and reporting, embedding strong analytics and performance insights to support better decision‑making.
Develop high‑performing teams and capability
Lead and develop a specialist team, while uplifting planning and system capability across the broader business through coaching, governance, and continuous improvement.

What you'll bring:

Experience & Qualifications

Bachelor's degree (or equivalent) in a relevant discipline.
10-15 years' experience in retail merchandise planning, inventory management, or related commercial roles.
Strong experience working with planning, forecasting, or retail ERP systems in complex environments.
Exposure to multi‑country, fast‑growth, or transformation‑led retail businesses.
Demonstrated experience leading change, system implementations, or operating model uplift.

Technical & Functional Expertise

Deep understanding of merchandise financial planning, forecasting, allocation, range planning, and lifecycle management.
Strong analytical capability, with confidence working with data, planning tools, and advanced Excel.
Proven ability to translate commercial strategy into system and process requirements.
Experience working cross‑functionally with technology, data, and supply chain teams.

Leadership & Ways of Working

Strong stakeholder leadership skills, with the ability to influence across functions and geographies.
Comfortable operating in ambiguity and fast‑paced environments.
A collaborative, commercially minded leader who balances strategic thinking with operational execution.
Passion for building scalable systems, improving planning discipline, and developing people capability

We are excited to have conversations with anyone who feels this could be the right next step in their career, so even if you don't tick all the boxes listed above, we'd still love for you to apply!

A place you can belong

At Anko we celebrate the rich diversity of the communities in which we operate and are committed to creating inclusive and safe environments where all our team members can contribute and succeed. We believe that all team members should feel valued, respected, and safe irrespective of your gender, ethnicity, indigeneity, religious beliefs, education, age, disability, family responsibilities, sexual orientation and gender identity and we encourage applications from all candidates.

We offer inclusive parental leave, and ongoing support offered though our partnership with Circle In for team members of all backgrounds and family circumstances

Great career opportunities across the broader Wesfarmers Group

Strengthen and embed transparent, rigorous investment approval processes

Excellent communication skills - able to translate complex analysis into clear recommendations.

Mission-led, we build passionate teams who support older people through life's most difficult circumstances.

With over 90 years of experience, we're known for our relationship-based approach. Whether it's in our homes, hospitals or the community, we bring skill, empathy and innovation to every moment of care.

It's not just care. It's HammondCare.

About the role

The role of Senior BI Developer is responsible for designing, developing and supporting on premises and cloud based system integrations across HammondCare's application and data landscape.


This is a permanent full-time role based at St Leonards with hybrid opportunities.

What we can offer you

We care about your wellbeing at HammondCare and as a result offer a wide range of health and lifestyle benefits including:

NFP salary packaging ($15,900 tax free) plus meals and entertainment benefit (up to $2,650 tax free)
Additional leave purchase options and 14 weeks paid parental leave
Flexible working options, including hybrid arrangements
Discounts on everyday products and services
Discounted healthcare memberships - access to network of fitness centres and pools for employees and families
Free access to independent counselling support for employees and families
Health & Wellbeing programmes
Reward & Recognition programmes
Referral bonuses
Career and professional development opportunities

Key Responsibilities include

Integration Design & Development

Design, develop, and maintain on-premises, cloud, and hybrid integrations across enterprise systems
Build and maintain integrations using a mix of: On Prem integration tools (e.g. SSIS), Microsoft Fabric components (e.g. Lakehouse, Data Pipelines, Notebooks) Cloud native services (e.g. Azure Data Factory, APIs) iPaaS platforms (e.g. Workato) for SaaS and event driven integrations
Develop API based, file based, database, and event driven integrations as required.
Ensure integration solutions align with enterprise architecture, security, and data governance standards

Data Quality, Reliability & Performance

Monitor and support integration workloads across on Prem and cloud environments
Implement robust error handling, logging, alerting, and retry mechanisms across all integration platforms
Troubleshoot and resolve issues spanning network boundaries, authentication models, and system dependencies

Collaboration & Stakeholder Engagement

Work closely with application owners, vendors, and business stakeholders to design fit for purpose integration solutions
Support integration between internal systems and third party SaaS platforms using various tools
Collaborate with business and Infrastructure teams to support end to end data flows

DevOps, Governance & Documentation

Use version control and CI/CD practices to manage integration assets across on prem and cloud platforms
Document integration architectures, data mappings, schedules, dependencies, and operational support procedures
Ensure integrations comply with security, privacy, and regulatory requirements

About you

Commitment to engage and align with HammondCare's Christian Mission, Motivation,
and Mission in Action.
Minimum 5 years' experience in a similar role in a fast paced environment
Relevant IT / Computing Science / Data Analytics / Data Science
Strong proficiency in SQL for data extraction, transformation, troubleshooting, and supporting integration and Fabric ingestion pipelines. (5+ Years)
Handson experience using Python for API integrations, data transformation, automation, validation, and bespoke processing, including use within Fabric notebooks where applicable. (5+ Years)
Practical experience with SSIS (on prem) and ADF (cloud) to build and support hybrid integration workflows. (3+ Years)
Experience using Workato (or similar iPaaS tools) to deliver SaaS, event driven, and work flow based integrations. (3+ Years)
Experience ingesting and preparing data in Microsoft Fabric (e.g. Lakehouse, pipelines/notebooks) to support Power BI and enterprise analytics. (3+ Years)
Post graduate qualifications in management and business administration or technology discipline (desirable)
Professional certifications in areas of project management, data architecture, ITIL, analytics or related accreditations demonstrating a commitment to lifelong learning (desirable)

To Apply

If you are ready to make a difference with HammondCare, Apply Now! For any specific queries please contact talent@hammond.com.au.

Pre- Employment Checks

All short-listed applicants for these positions will be asked to consent to a criminal record check and reference checking.

HammondCare is committed to creating an inclusive workplace that promotes and values diversity and inclusion. We strive on creating an equal employment environment where everyone from any background can be themselves.

This is a founding Data hire, a Lead Data/Analytics Engineer within a fast-growing, venture-backed SaaS company solving complex problems to make the world a better place! You will take full ownership from a hands on perspective in building the data function and platform from the ground up in a true greenfield environment.

The business already generates large volumes of data across multiple systems, but it is currently unstructured creating a high-impact opportunity to design a scalable, well-modelled data foundation. This is a hands-on individual contributor role with significant influence, working closely with leadership to shape how data is used across the organisation.

This position won't suit someone who has worked in large corporates for the entirety of their career - you should have experience in start up/scale up (unstructured) environments, you will be expected to lay the foundations for growth while working closely with the Co-Founder & Chief Product Officer.

Responsibilities
Design and build the end-to-end data platform from scratch
Develop and maintain scalable data pipelines and ETL processes
Create clean, well-structured data models and curated data layers
Translate complex business workflows into usable data structures
Partner with stakeholders to define data requirements and use cases
Establish best practices across data architecture, modelling, and governance
Enable analytics across product, growth, and operational functions
Consolidate and unify data from multiple sources into a single platform
Requirements
5+ years experience in Data Engineering or Analytics Engineering roles
Strong expertise in data modelling and dbt
Hands-on experience setting up greenfield Databricks (or similar) modern data platforms
Experience building data systems in greenfield or early-stage environments
Strong SQL and data warehousing fundamentals
Ability to work with ambiguity and translate business needs into technical solutions
Strong stakeholder engagement skills and commercial awareness
Experience with cloud platforms AWS/GCP, BigQuery etc..
What This Role Will Do for Your Career
Give you full ownership of a data function from day one
Provide the opportunity to design and implement a modern data stack end-to-end
Develop deep expertise in solving complex, real-world data problems
Position you as a senior technical leader without needing to manage a team
Offer exposure to high-growth scaling challenges in a fast-moving environment
Enable close collaboration with leadership on business-critical decisions

Please note**

- This position requires Sydney based candidates, willing to work 3 days on-site in Sydney CBD

- You must be Australian Permanent Resident or Citizen

- Only those who meet the above criteria will be considered

Our purpose

Here at Datacom, we connect people and technology in order to solve challenges, create opportunities and discover new possibilities for the communities we live in.

About the Role

We are seeking a Senior Technical Business Analyst with very strong API and integration experience to join our high-performing, agile teams. This is a hands-on, technically-focused BA role in a multinational environment, working with diverse clients, high-paced delivery, and modern engineering practices.

You will operate as the key bridge between business stakeholders and technical teams, with a strong emphasis on API-driven solutions, integration design, and scalable, secure services. You’ll work closely with architects, developers, and testers to ensure that business and technical requirements are clearly defined, well-documented, and successfully delivered.

If you enjoy working on complex integration initiatives, API ecosystems and cloud-native platforms, and you want to extend your impact across new clients, teams and technologies, this role is for you.

What you’ll do:

Requirements & Analysis

Understand client business domains, existing processes, systems and integration landscapes.
Lead requirements discovery and analysis for API and integration-focused projects.
Elicit and document functional and non-functional requirements (including performance, security, resilience).
Translate business requirements into detailed technical specifications for APIs, integrations and data flows.
Create and maintain:
Jira tickets / user stories with clear, testable acceptance criteria.
API and integration requirements, mapping documents and interface specifications.
UML/sequence diagrams and integration design patterns.
Mapping tables, data contracts and field-level mappings between consumers and providers.
Environment and endpoint documentation, Postman collections, and related assets in Confluence.

API & Integration Focus

Lead the analysis and design of REST and SOAP integrations, with a strong bias toward modern RESTful APIs.
Design and maintain API contracts (Swagger/OpenAPI) and govern changes across consumers and providers.
Work with teams using:
Spring Boot and Java-based microservices 
Netflix OSS stack (Zuul, Ribbon, Eureka) or similar service discovery and gateway solutions.
Help define API standards, best practices and reuse patterns across the platform.
Contribute to API security, error handling, resilience, and observability requirements.
Collaborate on messaging and streaming patterns (e.g. ActiveMQ, Kafka and similar event/queue-based systems).
Support performance, load and integration testing (e.g. Rest Assured, Gatling, Hoverfly) by ensuring test scenarios align with business and technical requirements.

Delivery & Collaboration

Work in an agile (Scrum/SaFe) environment and actively participate in:
Backlog refinement, sprint planning, and reviews.
3 Amigos sessions (Developer, Tester, BA) to clarify requirements and acceptance criteria.
Workshops and stakeholder sessions for requirements, design and prioritisation.
Collaborate closely with:
Technical Leads and Architects to ensure solution and integration architecture align with business needs.
Developers to resolve specification queries and clarify integration behaviour.
Test Analysts to ensure coverage of functional and non-functional requirements, and awareness of changes, issues, and risks.
Provide effort estimates for change requests and larger pieces of work using a clear, traceable estimation approach.
Contribute to and/or lead the production, review and signoff of:
Requirements documentation.
Functional and integration design documents.
User guides and operational handover materials as needed.
Ensure all required project reviews and checkpoints are completed to a high standard.

Technical & Conceptual Skills

You bring strong, hands-on experience and understanding across:

Toolkit / Tools

Confluence & Jira.
Swagger / OpenAPI (API contract design and maintenance).
Postman, Rest Assured, SOAP UI for API and integration testing.
Spring Boot, Java 11+/17+, Gradle (awareness of how services are built and deployed).
Netflix Stack (Zuul, Ribbon, Eureka) or equivalent discovery/gateway components.
Splunk or similar logging/observability tools.
Git/Bitbucket, IntelliJ/Eclipse or similar IDEs.
Additional testing and simulation tools (e.g. Gatling, Hoverfly) desirable.

Concepts

REST APIs and modern API design principles.
API security (authentication, authorisation, OAuth2/OpenID Connect, tokens).
API best practices and APIs at scale (versioning, governance, rate limiting, backwards compatibility).
Reusability and modular service design.
Integration patterns and flows (sync/async, messaging, pub/sub, request–reply).
Error handling and resilience patterns.
Service discovery and microservices architecture.
Containerisation and cloud concepts (deployment, environments, scalability).
Distributed tracing, logging, monitoring and observability.
CI/CD pipeline awareness and how it impacts requirements and releases.
Agile and SAFe concepts and ceremonies.
ITSM practices around change and release (awareness).

Professional Qualities

5+ years’ experience as a Technical Business Analyst in agile teams, with a strong emphasis on APIs and integrations.
Proven track record working closely with development and architecture teams on API-driven solutions.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with high attention to detail.
Comfortable working in fast-paced, multi-team, multi-stakeholder environments.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills; able to translate between business and technical audiences.
Deadline-driven, proactive, and able to own outcomes from discovery through to delivery.
Collaborative, team-oriented, and confident facilitating workshops and discussions.
Proficient in English.

About Datacom

We have over 6,200 people across our global offices, and we have extensive expertise in operating data centres, providing IT services, software engineering and application management, as well as payroll and customer service design and operations. With this comes a long list of significant clients Datacom is committed to hiring, developing and promoting the best talent from a diverse range of backgrounds. We are local at heart, yet world-class in capability.

About QIC

As one of Australias leading institutional investment managers with more than $100 billion in assets under management, we have a presence across four continents, seven offices and assets across global markets.

At QIC, we believe in shared value - where delivering strong investment returns can go hand in hand with doing good for our clients, people and communities. If we invest well, we will all do better together.

Life at QIC

We know that our people perform at their best when they feel a sense of belonging and are encouraged to bring their authentic selves to work every day.  We provide a supportive work environment where everyone can thrive and feel valued for their meaningful contribution in delivering for the prosperity of our clients, people and communities.

Joining QIC, you can contribute to our employee led networks that champion our diversity, equity and inclusion strategy, which consists of five key pillars: Gender Equality, LGBTQ+, Accessibility, Cultural & Linguistic Diversity, and First Nations People.

Other reasons to join us:
Flexibility and hybrid options encouraged – certified Family Inclusive Workplace
Recharge leave - additional 3 days leave per year
Annual Fitness/home office reimbursement
Ability to swap public holidays for those of personal, cultural or religious significance
26 weeks gender-neutral paid parental leave
Financial study assistance and study leave
Community leave for supporting the causes you love
Development opportunities to extend, expand and enhance your growth.
At QIC, we welcome and encourage applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people, the LGBTQI+ community, people with disabilities and people from all cultural backgrounds. We have an equitable, barrier-free application process so please inform us of your accessibility requirements in your application or you can reach out to our Talent Team on 07 3360 3800.

About the role

This is not your average trading role. At QIC, you’ll be part of a 24-hour global execution platform, working across asset classes including equities, fixed interest, commodities, FX, and derivatives. You’ll execute trades with precision, contribute to execution research, and help drive innovation in trading processes.

This role reports to the Senior Manager, Trading – Liquid Markets Group and works closely with the Head of Portfolio Implementation. You’ll be well-supported by experienced leaders who are passionate about collaboration and delivering great outcomes for clients.

You’ll work closely with Portfolio Managers, Risk & Compliance, and external counterparties to ensure best execution and deliver value to our clients. This is a hands-on, fast-paced role where every day is different and every trade counts.

The team is known for its close-knit, collaborative culture where staff longevity speaks volumes, and many team members have built long-term careers within the group. It’s a supportive environment that fosters professional development through shared success, mutual respect, and a genuine enjoyment of working together.

Key responsibilities:

Execute trades across multiple asset classes with precision and compliance.

Contribute to transaction cost analysis and execution research.

Build and maintain strong relationships with counterparties and internal teams.

Support data management and reporting tools for analytics and client reporting.

Monitor market intelligence and regulatory developments.

Assist with trade preparation, authorisation, and audit activities.

Drive innovation and process improvement across trading operations.

About you

You’re curious, analytical, and thrive in a dynamic environment. You bring institutional trading desk experience and a strong grasp of database management and analytics. You’re collaborative, detail-oriented, and not afraid to roll up your sleeves.

To succeed in this role, you will have:

Experience in institutional trading, ideally across global markets including options.

Strong analytical and technical skills, including database management.

A collaborative mindset and excellent communication skills.

Ability to support offshore trading hours on a rotational basis.

A proactive attitude and high attention to detail.

Relevant tertiary qualifications (finance, commerce, engineering, etc.).

Prior experience trading equity options and familiarity with platforms such as Bloomberg, Tradeweb, or BlackRock Solutions and SQL and Python coding skills will be highly regarded. QIC is a place where careers grow, ideas thrive, and people stick around. With a culture built on respect, challenge, and fun, QIC offers more than just a job—it’s a place to build your future.

Job description:

Your role

We are seeking an experienced SAP Analytics Lead to drive analytics strategy, design, and delivery across SAP and enterprise data platforms. This role will work closely with business stakeholders, enterprise architects, and delivery teams to convert business requirements into scalable, insight driven analytics solutions.

Key Responsibilities

Own and drive the SAP analytics vision and roadmap aligned to business priorities.
Act as the Analytics Lead for SAP programs, ensuring architecture, design, and delivery quality.
Partner with business leaders to translate reporting, planning, and analytics needs into actionable solutions.
Lead and mentor analytics teams (onshore/offshore), fostering best practices and delivery excellence.
Contribute to thought leadership, pre sales activities, and client roadmap discussions.
Design and govern end to end analytics architectures using:
o SAP Analytics Cloud (Reporting, Planning, Predictive)
o SAP Datasphere (Business Data Cloud / BDC)
o Define data models, semantic layers, and data flows from SAP S/4HANA and non SAP sources.
Ensure alignment with enterprise data, security, and governance standards.
Guide performance optimisation, scalability, and future ready design.
Lead requirements workshops, solution design sessions, and design authority reviews.
Oversee build, testing, deployment, and hypercare activities.
Ensure high quality deliverables, on time milestones, and effective risk management.
Support UAT, business sign off, and smooth transition to operations.
Act as the primary analytics point of contact for senior client stakeholders.
Provide clear status, risks, and mitigation plans to program leadership.
Collaborate with SAP functional, integration, and security teams.



Profile description:

Your profile
Strong hands on and leadership experience with:
SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) - stories, planning models, live & acquired connectivity
SAP BW/4HANA
SAP Datasphere (BDC)
Experience integrating SAP and non SAP data sources.
Solid understanding of data modelling concepts (star schemas, analytical views, semantic layers).
Knowledge of SAP S/4HANA data structures and analytics enablement.
10+ years in analytics / BI with significant SAP focus.
Proven experience leading teams and large scale SAP analytics programs.
Ability to influence architecture decisions and guide executives.
Experience in consulting or complex enterprise client environments is highly desirable.

Desirable Experience

SAP Certified in SAC, BDC, or Datasphere.
Experience with planning and forecasting use cases in SAC.
Exposure to cloud data platforms (Azure, AWS, GCP) and advanced analytics.
Pre sales, proposal development, or solution estimation experience.


We offer:



What you'll love about working here



You will be a part of a diverse collective of free-thinkers, entrepreneurs and industry experts. You will love the exposure to the scale of transformation, the depth of expertise, and the opportunities for growth.
We aim to build an environment where employees can enjoy a positive work-life balance. We embed hybrid working in all that we do and make flexible working arrangements the day-to-day reality for our people.
At the heart of our mission is your career growth. You will have countless learning and development opportunities from thinktanks to hackathons, and access to 250,000 courses with numerous external certifications crafted to support you in exploring a world of opportunities.
We realise a Total Reward package should be more than just compensation. We offer a range of core and flexible benefits and have a Peer Recognition Portal called ‘Celebrate'.
We don't just believe in inclusion; we actively go out to making it a working reality. Driven by our core values and ‘Inclusive future for all' campaign, we build environments where you can bring your whole self to work.

Apply today!

We’re seeking a highly experienced Technical Business Analyst across Wellington, Auckland, Melbourne, and Sydney to join a large‑scale, multi‑year transformation program modernising customer communications across enterprise platforms. This strategic initiative impacts millions of customer interactions every year, with a committed roadmap that offers long‑term engagement, ongoing innovation, and the chance to work on highly complex, meaningful problems.

With strong SmartCOMM expertise, you’ll play a critical role in bridging business needs and technical solutions—working closely with clients, developers, and QA teams to shape and deliver SmartCOMM‑based customer communication solutions end‑to‑end. From analysing complex integration scenarios to defining functional and technical specifications, you’ll be hands‑on throughout the delivery lifecycle. You’ll thrive in a dynamic, client‑facing environment, collaborating with a highly energetic team and contributing to Agile ways of working while delivering solutions that genuinely improve customer communication at scale.

This role sits within our Information Services practice, one of four key areas at Tenzing, alongside Technology Services, Management Consulting, and Managed Services. The Information Services team partners with clients to deliver all things data including data science, data warehousing, and data migrations. We use proven methodologies along with industry-leading technology (like Snowflake!) to create scalable and sustainable data solutions tailored to our customer’s needs.

What you bring

You'll have proven ability in:

Business analysis in technical environments (at least 5 years' experience)

Working with SmartComm or similar CCM platforms

SmartComm qualification and practical delivery experience is a must!

Gathering and documenting business and technical requirements

Designing and validating customer communication workflows and document templates

Collaborating with developers and QA teams to ensure successful delivery

Understanding of REST APIs and integration patterns

Agile methodologies and facilitation skills

It’s important you have a minimum of three years’ experience in a consultancy, a strong background in application QA or development, and the ability to work independently. You’ll also be skilled in documentation, confident in estimating testing effort, and delivering work in line with those estimates.


Why join Tenzing, who are we?

At Tenzing, we’ve been helping clients scale their mountains for over twenty years. We call it the Sherpa ethos - standing alongside our clients, doing the heavy lifting, and guiding them through complex transformations. As trusted partners in change, we work with some of Australasia’s leading organisations to deliver strategic and operational impact across industries.
Tenzing is an independent subsidiary of the Tech Mahindra Group, giving our people access to a global network of expertise and opportunities, while maintaining our well-known Tenzing ways of working and deep understanding of the local context across New Zealand and Australia.

We believe our people are our greatest asset. That’s why we invest in tailored career development, mentorship, and a collaborative environment where creativity and professionalism thrive. We also take the time to celebrate our wins, recognising individual and team achievements, and fostering a culture of appreciation and shared success.
Our leadership team is made up of highly experienced professionals who are passionate about what they do. They are deeply knowledgeable, approachable, and committed to empowering their teams, encouraging individuals to take on new challenges, grow their capabilities, and make a meaningful impact. Come meet them!

What we offer:

Competitive Salary

Discretionary annual bonus

Flexible and hybrid working options.

Ability to purchase additional annual leave.

Life and Income Protection Insurance (fully subsidised)

Discounted Medical Insurance

Comprehensive Learning & Development programmes

Wellbeing support including EAP and flu vaccinations

Inclusive culture with regular social events and celebrations

If you're ready to work on meaningful projects, expand your technical toolkit, and grow within a consulting environment, we’d love to hear from you.

Job Title: Clinical Coding Specialist - Health Information Manager
Ad Text:
About the Role

We are seeking an experienced Clinical Coding Specialist / Health Information Manager to join our Code Focus team and play a key role in shaping how clinical coding expertise is embedded into systems used by hospitals across Australia.

In this role, you will contribute to the ongoing evolution and optimisation of Code Focus and its associated modules. You will apply your clinical coding expertise to ensure system guidance, rules, and prompts remain current, practical, and aligned with national standards and industry advice, supporting efficient coding workflows and strong funding outcomes for health services.

This role is well suited to someone who enjoys working at the intersection of health information and systems and is comfortable using digital platforms to embed subject matter expertise into practical, system-driven solutions. You will collaborate closely with clients, internal product and development teams, and business stakeholders, providing informed guidance and contributing to considered product and enhancement decisions.

Key Responsibilities

Provide clinical coding subject matter expertise to support the ongoing development and refinement of Code Focus features, including system guidance, notifications, and prompts

Review and incorporate updates from ICD-10-AM editions, ACS, ACHI, and relevant industry advice into the platform

Coordinate and synthesise clinical coding guidance from recognised industry sources for inclusion within Code Focus

Engage with existing clients to understand requirements, triage requests, and support prioritisation of enhancements and incidents

Support the implementation of Code Focus at new health services, including configuration, validation, and advisory support

Assist with coordination and prioritisation of service desk enquiries

Work with the Business Development Manager to articulate product value, points of difference, and business outcomes to prospective clients

Act as an internal Application Specialist and Subject Matter Expert across clinical coding, casemix, and funding models

Required Skills and Experience

Minimum 5 years’ clinical coding experience, ideally including time as a Coding Educator and/or Auditor

Strong working knowledge of ICD-10-AM, ACHI, and ACS, including completion of Thirteenth Edition education

Experience working with health information systems, including exposure to system administration, configuration, implementation, or enhancement activities within hospital or healthcare environments

Sound understanding of hospital workflows, Health Information Systems, and coding operations

Demonstrated knowledge of activity-based funding and casemix principles

Clear, professional written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to present information and provide considered advice to a range of stakeholders

An understanding of the software development lifecycle, with the ability to work effectively with product and development teams to translate clinical and business requirements into system outcomes

Strong self-management skills and the ability to work collaboratively within a multidisciplinary team

Qualifications

Clinical Coding qualification, Bachelor of Health Information Management, or equivalent professional experience

Desirable Experience

Experience coding across both public and private health services

Exposure to interstate funding models, including National Weighted Activity Units (NWAU)

Understanding of alternative funding models such as ANSNAP and mental health

Foundation knowledge of health system integration concepts, including HL7 messaging

Familiarity with emerging AI-enabled clinical coding workflows

Project or implementation experience within health information or digital health environments.

About Data Agility

Data Agility is a specialist analytics and information management firm delivering AI and analytics solutions alongside data governance, quality, migration, integration, reporting, and the strategies that bring these capabilities together. Since 2003, we have partnered with organisations to apply trusted data in practical ways to address business challenges and improve performance.

Our specialist Health Practice has delivered over 300 projects nationally and is recognised for its comprehensive understanding of health sector ICT environments, including the complexities of system implementation, integration, ongoing operation, and support.

About Code Focus

Code Focus is Data Agility’s flagship product, and the leading Australian end-to-end HIM coding lifecycle and workflow automation platform created by Health Information Managers for Health Information Managers.

Used by public and private health services across Australia, Code Focus supports the complete clinical coding lifecycle, enabling health services to accurately capture CDI activity and impact, optimise funding outcomes, and make informed, data-driven operational and financial decisions. Through intelligent automation, Code Focus improves efficiency across HIM teams, with each implementation configured to suit the health service's specific needs using AI-powered Encoder and Grouper, Workflow Management, Real Time Coder Feedback and Validation, Auditing and Clinical Documentation Improvement (CDI) solutions, underpinned by more than 20 years of health information expertise.

Why Join Us

This role offers the opportunity to work with a well-established product used by health services across Australia, alongside a highly experienced team with deep sector knowledge. You will have meaningful influence over how clinical coding guidance is embedded into technology, supporting better outcomes for both coders and health services.

Own and uplift Specialist Incentive Plans across our commercial divisions
Make your mark by influencing Executives on specialised reward, risk and performance
High-impact, visible role blending strategy with delivery in a regulated environment

 

Our people are customer obsessed. They prioritise the needs and satisfaction of the customer above all else. Our mindset fosters innovation and creates strong, lasting customer relationships as we strive to be the most customer centric company in Australia and New Zealand.

As the Senior Consultant, Performance and Reward, you will be part of our Rewards team that is doubling down on customer obsession.

Each day, you’ll go above and beyond to: 

Lead the design, delivery and ongoing optimisation of Specialist Incentive Plans for key revenue generating roles across our commercial divisions
Partner closely with Business, Finance, Risk and People & Culture colleagues to ensure incentive plans are commercially effective, risk-aligned and operationally deliverable
Ensure Sedgwick, CPS 511 and relevant governance committee requirements are met
Be the link between business strategy, performance outcomes and reward, ensuring incentive frameworks drive the right behaviours, customer outcomes and long-term value creation
Ensure reward decisions meet regulatory, risk and enterprise requirements.

 

We’re looking for the best and brightest to deliver for our customers. You’ll need:

Advanced Reward expertise across remuneration frameworks, incentive design, governance and performance management within tier 1 banking essential
Demonstrated experience leading the design, delivery and ongoing optimisation of Specialist Incentive Plans for revenue-generating roles
Ability to translate complex data, regulatory, risk and remuneration concepts into practical business solutions
Strong stakeholder management and influencing skills to shape outcomes and achieve strategic initiatives

About the Position

The Department is developing the Research Insights Capability as a new approach to national research evaluation. The capability is designed to generate flexible, data-driven insights into the research system through analytical dashboards, reports and State of the Research Environment publications.

The project aims to strengthen understanding of the Australian research system, recognise excellence, support evidence-based policy development, and ensure insights generated through evaluation activities are accessible to stakeholders while minimising reporting burden on universities. To support the development and implementation of the Research Insights Capability, the department requires dedicated capability to design evaluation frameworks, translate policy concepts into measurable indicators, and integrate analytical outputs with the departments data systems.

Duties and Tasks

The Assistant Manager - Evaluation Policy will contribute to several areas supporting the Research Insights Capability.

Evaluation Framework Development:

Support the development of evaluation approaches aligned with the Research Insights Capability
Contribute to the design of indicators across research excellence, impact and research depth.
Indicator Development:

Translate evaluation policy concepts into measurable indicators
Work with the departments internal data teams to identify relevant datasets and test indicator feasibility

Research System Analysis:


Analyse the Australian research landscape to understand anomalies and trends
Examine collaboration patterns between researchers, institutions and end-users

Analytical Reporting:

Prepare analytical reports, briefings and insights

International Engagement:

Support the departments engagement with international initiatives on research evaluation
Maintain awareness of global developments in responsible research assessment, including collaborating with Research on Research Institute and maintaining awareness of emerging projects

Required Skills and Knowledge

strong understanding of research evaluation policy
demonstrated ability to translate policy objectives into measurable indicators and frameworks
experience supporting evaluation and classification frameworks the ability to work with data scientists to translate technical outputs into the policy outcomes
strong analytical judgement and the ability to interpret complex information to inform the decision-making of senior executives
experience preparing high-quality briefs, reports and advice for senior executives and stakeholders
strong stakeholder engagement skills across government, research and external sectors
awareness of international developments in research evaluation
highly developed written and verbal communication skills

Our Ideal Candidate

strong experience in policy development and evaluation frameworks.
able to translate complex policy concepts into practical outputs that can be implemented within a national evaluation framework.
comfortable working across strategic policy, analysis and delivery,
demonstrated experience contributing to evaluation policy and classification systems within a government context.
track record of strong analytical judgement and the ability to navigate complex stakeholder environments.
experienced in providing clear, well-reasoned advice to senior decision-makers, and will have demonstrated the ability to be collaborative, outcome-focused
capable of leading work across teams to deliver high-quality, policy-relevant outputs.

LIFECYCLE & RETENTION MARKETING MANAGER

THE ROLE:

Bared Footwear presently operates in six markets (AU, NZ, UK, SG, HK, US) and is building a full-funnel growth ecosystem under a newly created Head of Growth function. This role owns the customer lifecycle from first contact through repeat purchase and advocacy, with the goal of turning one-time buyers into high-LTV repeat customers.

This is not a campaign scheduling role. Yes, you will build and optimise flows and campaigns daily. But your mandate is broader: establish our CDP capability, design the segmentation and data architecture that powers personalisation at scale, and create the feedback loop between retention and acquisition that makes every marketing dollar work harder. You will think in terms of CLV, repeat rate, ERFM and owned channel contribution, not just deliverability and click-throughs.

You will work in lockstep with the Demand & Acquisition Manager and closely with Marketing, eComm, Tech, Ops, Retail and Customer Service teams to ensure customer data is accurate, actionable and consistently leveraged across all channels.


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:

Lifecycle strategy and execution

Own the end-to-end lifecycle roadmap across email, SMS and emerging channels (push, WhatsApp) for all markets.

Design, build and optimise automated flows in Klaviyo: welcome, post-purchase, browse/cart abandonment, winback, VIP/loyalty, sunset, cross-sell, upsell, etc.

Evolve toward behaviour-triggered, personalised journeys informed by purchase history, intent and predicted CLV.

Partner with Marketing on EDM strategy, segmentation, testing plans and list builds. Act as the final quality checkpoint for all CRM-driven sends.


Customer data and governance

Evaluate, select and activate the right solution to unify customer profiles, configure identity resolution and establish a single source of truth across all storefronts (CDP or composable via Data Warehouse).

Implement RFM analysis, predictive CLV modelling, funnel analysis and product analytics.

Build and maintain advanced segmentation using behavioural, transactional and predictive attributes. Define and maintain Ideal Customer Profiles (ICPs).

Establish data governance standards: naming conventions, event taxonomy, profile hygiene and consent management across six markets.

Oversee list quality, govern acquisition sources (forms, zero party collection, campaigns) and manage onsite capture points.

Stay informed on global privacy regulations (GDPR, Australian Privacy Act, PDPA etc.) and ensure CRM/CDP/Data Warehouse systems strictly honour consent requirements.

Loyalty and retention

Support the development and optimisation of loyalty marketing and retention initiatives.

Use ERFM insights to identify opportunities to improve repeat purchase rate, engagement and lifetime value.

Ensure loyalty segments and behaviours are accurately captured and accessible across systems. Track programme performance and recommend enhancements.


Growth ecosystem integration

Build suppression and exclusion audiences to improve paid media efficiency and reduce wasted spend on existing customers.

Create high-value seed audiences from lifecycle segments for lookalike targeting across paid channels.

Work in lockstep with the Demand & Acquisition Manager to ensure retention data feeds acquisition strategy: suppression, seed audiences and customer intelligence that makes every paid dollar work harder.

What You'll Bring
