Job Title: Senior Data Engineer – Melbourne - $180,000 - Gain "productionised" AI
Ad Text:
Senior Data Engineer – Melbourne - $180,000 - Gain "productionised" Agentic AI and Gen AI 


Morgan Consulting are partnering with an exciting and fast growing organisation seeking a Senior Data Engineer to join their Data function. This is an opportunity to gain Agentic and Generative AI "Productionised" experience. This role offers strong Career Development within an organisation which operates with very little red-tape and has a supportive and positive culture.

About the Role:

The Senior Data Engineer is a hands on technical specialist responsible for building and supporting modern Databricks Lakehouse solutions on Azure within a complex retail data landscape. Working across both project initiatives and BAU, the role delivers robust, scalable data pipelines and curated analytical datasets that underpin enterprise BI, advanced analytics, and AI. You will collaborate closely with retail stakeholders, BI developers, and broader technology teams to transform high volume retail data into trusted, production ready insights that support critical business outcomes. This is an opportunity to gain Agentic and Generative AI "Productionised" experience.

What You Will Do:

Platform & Analytics Enablement

Enable enterprise BI, analytics, and AI driven insights by delivering scalable, high‑quality data solutions that support retail trading, operational, and customer use cases.
Build and optimise data pipelines and integrations that underpin reporting, advanced analytics, and AI/ML workloads.
Ensure analytics and reporting solutions are performant, secure, scalable, and fit for enterprise and retail decision making.

Azure & Databricks Data Engineering

Design, develop, and evolve modern data engineering solutions on Microsoft Azure, with a strong focus on Azure Databricks, Azure Data Factory (ADF), and Azure Data Lake Storage (ADLS).
Develop robust data transformations using PySpark, Databricks SQL, SQL, and Python.
Design, implement, and maintain curated analytical and dimensional data models aligned to retail BI, analytics, and AI requirements.

BI & Semantic Layer Delivery

Develop, optimise, and support Power BI datasets, semantic models, and reports to deliver trusted, business ready insights.
Administer Power BI environments, including workspace management, refresh orchestration, security, and access controls.
Align BI solutions to best practice data modelling, performance optimisation, and usability standards.

Production Support & Operational Excellence

Provide BAU support across data platforms, pipelines, BI, and analytics solutions, including issue resolution and root cause analysis.
Monitor data platforms and pipelines, implementing logging, alerting, and performance tuning.
Proactively identify and resolve data quality, availability, and performance issues in production environments.

Engineering Standards, DevOps & Security

Implement and maintain CI/CD pipelines for data and analytics solutions using Azure DevOps.
Apply cloud security, authentication, and access control best practices across Azure data and analytics services.
Contribute to design reviews, code reviews, and technical discussions to ensure robust, maintainable, and scalable solutions.
Support the definition and continuous improvement of data engineering, analytics, and BI standards.

Governance & Collaboration

Contribute to the design, adoption, and communication of data governance practices across the data platform.
Partner closely with retail business stakeholders to translate requirements into reliable technical solutions.
Collaborate with engineering, data science, infrastructure, and DevOps teams to deliver secure, production ready solutions.
Balance delivery across project initiatives and ongoing BAU activities within a complex retail data environment.


What You Will Bring:


Strong hands on Data Engineering experience with SQL, Python, PySpark and Databricks SQL.
Strong experience designing and delivering data solutions on Microsoft Azure - Azure Databricks, Azure Data Factory (ADF), Azure Data Lake Storage (ADLS).
Proven experience designing and building data warehouses and analytical data models, including scalable lakehouse and dimensional modelling patterns.
Strong understanding of data integration, analytics platforms, and reporting architectures in enterprise environments.
Hands‑on experience administering and developing Power BI solutions, including Dataset and semantic model design, Strong DAX capability, Performance optimisation and governance.
Experience working in DevOps environments, including - CI/CD pipelines, Source control, Release and environment management.
General understanding of cloud authentication, security, and access control concepts, including role‑based access and data security principles.
Demonstrated ability to deliver both project work and BAU/support, operating confidently in a production data and BI environment.
Proven retail sector Data Engineering experience to Senior Data Engineer level.

Why Join:

Opportunity to gain Agentic and Generative AI "Productionised" experience.
Strong Career Development.
Amazing culture with very little red tape.

If you are a Senior Data Engineer with strong stakeholder management, who has experience in a fast-paced retail environment, and is outcome focussed we would love to hear from you. Apply now or reach out for a confidential conversation.

At the centre of the Wesfarmers Group, we are building a cross-skilled and diverse technology team focused on engaging the business, understanding strategic drivers, co-creating value-adding solutions and pushing technical boundaries to innovate and deliver excellence to the Corporate Office.

As Senior AI Transformation Analyst, you are accountable for identifying, analysing, and packaging AI transformation opportunities across the Wesfarmers Corporate Office. Working directly with business teams, you will develop a deep understanding of how each function operates, where friction and inefficiency exist, and how AI can meaningfully change the way work gets done.

You will translate business needs into well-defined AI opportunity briefs that the AI delivery team can act on, bridging the gap between business intent and technical execution. This requires equal comfort operating in business conversations and in the AI tool landscape, with the credibility to guide teams through what is possible and what is practical.

Working at the interface between the business and the AI delivery team, you will ensure that transformation opportunities are well understood, clearly defined, and ready to build. Once solutions are in place, you will support the business through the transition to new ways of working, helping teams build the confidence and capability needed to get lasting value from AI capabilities.

This is an 18 month max-term position based in Perth.

What You’ll Do

•                    Engage with Corporate Office functions to develop a thorough understanding of current processes, ways of working, pain points, and strategic priorities.

•                    Identify and assess opportunities where AI can deliver meaningful transformation, efficiency, or quality improvements across functions including Finance, Legal, HR, Corporate Affairs, and others.

•                    Conduct structured discovery sessions with function leads and team members to surface AI use cases, prioritise them by value and feasibility, and build a pipeline of transformation opportunities for delivery.

•                    Develop well-structured AI opportunity briefs and business requirements that give the Manager, AI Delivery and Senior AI Engineers the context they need to design and build effective solutions.

•                    Act as the primary liaison between the business function and the AI delivery team throughout the delivery lifecycle, providing clarity on requirements and facilitating decisions where needed.

•                    Develop change management plans for AI transformation initiatives, considering the people, process, and capability dimensions of each change.

•                    Maintain a current and practical understanding of the enterprise AI tool landscape, including Microsoft 365 Copilot, Claude, ChatGPT Enterprise, and Google Gemini, with the ability to explain their capabilities and appropriate use cases to non-technical audiences.

•                    Build trusted relationships with leaders and team members across Corporate Office functions, acting as a credible and practical advisor on AI transformation.

 

What You’ll Bring

•                    Demonstrated experience in a business analysis, process improvement, or transformation role within a complex organisation.

•                    Working knowledge of the enterprise AI tool landscape, along with practical exposure to AI tools and GenAI platforms in a professional context, with the ability to assess their applicability to business problems.

•                    Experience facilitating discovery workshops and engaging stakeholders at various levels to elicit and document requirements.

•                    Experience designing or supporting change management programs, including stakeholder engagement, communication planning, and adoption tracking.

•                    Exposure to working alongside technical delivery teams, with an understanding of how business requirements flow into design and build activities.

•                    Excellent written communication skills, with the ability to produce clear and well-structured briefing documents, opportunity assessments, and executive materials.

•                    Strong interpersonal and relationship-building skills, with the ability to build trust quickly across diverse teams.

•                    Tertiary qualified in relevant technology discipline (Solution Architecture, Business Improvement, Artificial Intelligence, Data Science, Mathematics, Computer Science).

•                    Lean or 6 Sigma equivalent certification preferred Work experience.

 

Technology used

•                    Microsoft 365 suite including Copilot.

•                    Enterprise GenAI tools (Claude, ChatGPT Enterprise, Google Gemini).

•                    Process mapping and diagramming tools (Visio, Lucidchart or equivalent).

•                    Process automation tools (UIPath, Kognitos or equivalent).

•                    Project and task management platforms.

 

About Wesfarmers

As one of Australia’s largest listed companies, we’re proud of our longstanding contribution to Australians.

We continually reinvent ourselves and invest in the businesses we own and operate, including leading retailers like Bunnings, Kmart, Target, Officeworks and Priceline. We also have an online offering, including OnePass and the Group shared data asset.

Our work doesn’t stop there, we operate in the industrials sector which supplies the nation with chemicals, energy and fertilisers, and industrial and safety products. And we participate in an integrated lithium joint venture, including the operation of a mine and concentrator and the development of a refinery.

Join the team at Corporate Office which provides support and advice across the Wesfarmers Group

The Corporate Office provides corporate services and support to the autonomous businesses within the Wesfarmers Group, enabling the achievement of Group objectives. It leads capital allocation and portfolio management for the Group and focuses on leveraging and enhancing the Group’s reputation to support attraction and retention of talent, securing investment opportunities and maintaining a low-cost capital.

To do this, we need great people, working together and role modelling our values and ways of working.

Detailed research analysis experience with ASX200 companies

Prior experience on AGM and EGM voting and reporting

Key Requirements:

Strong analytical skills and attention to detail

Strong organisational and time management abilities

Excellent stakeholder management, communication and interpersonal skills

Management or leadership experience

About the role

Support capital raising initiatives and fund establishment activities
Assist with due diligence processes across investment and financing transactions
Monitor debt facilities and support lender engagement and debt management activities
Support BAU capital management initiatives across the group

Partner with finance and investment teams across international offices

Degree qualified in Accounting, Finance, Economics or a related discipline
Experience within corporate finance, transactions, funds or investment environments
Exposure to capital raising, fund structures or transaction due diligence highly desirable
Strong financial analysis and financial modelling capability
Confident communicator comfortable engaging with senior stakeholders
Ability to operate in a fast paced, commercially focused environment

Join a leading global IT consulting firm delivering large-scale digital transformation programmes across the utilities sector. This is a hands-on SAP CPI role focused on building robust, scalable integrations across complex enterprise landscapes.

You'll work on end-to-end integration design, API-led connectivity, and middleware orchestration across SAP and non-SAP systems in a high-impact environment.

What's in it for you:

- Work on enterprise-wide integration programmes within the utilities sector - complex, high-value transformation work
- Join a globally recognised consultancy with strong pipeline, career growth, and long-term project stability
- Hands-on ownership of CPI design, build and optimisation across modern cloud-based SAP landscapes

The Role:

Design and deliver integration solutions using SAP CPI / Integration Suite, building and maintaining iFlows across diverse systems. You'll work closely with functional teams and vendors to translate business requirements into scalable integration architectures, while supporting deployment, monitoring and optimisation across environments.

Key Responsibilities:

- Design end-to-end integration architecture using SAP CPI / Integration Suite
- Develop and maintain iFlows using Groovy, XML/XSLT mappings, and SAP CPI tooling
- Configure integrations across REST, SOAP, OData, SFTP, IDoc and JDBC
- Manage security including OAuth, certificates, keystores and authentication protocols
- Support testing cycles (SIT/UAT) and manage deployments across environments
- Monitor and troubleshoot integrations, providing L2/L3 support
- Collaborate with SAP functional teams and external vendors

Skills & Experience:

- Strong SAP CPI / Integration Suite experience
- Solid background in API-led integration and middleware design
- Experience with XML, JSON, XSLT, XPath and Groovy scripting
- Strong understanding of REST/SOAP services and API design
- Exposure to SAP Cloud Foundry, IDocs, BAPIs, RFCs and OData
- Experience with authentication methods including OAuth2, SAML and certificates
- Utilities domain experience highly desirable

The Brand Protection Analyst, assigned to a specific client, will be responsible for producing monthly enforcement reports that detail trademark infringement analyses, outcomes from test buys, and takedown actions. This role involves organizing intellectual property (IP) files and documentation for legal support, including budget forecasts and invoice summaries, and preparing training materials and compliance communications for suppliers and stakeholders. The work schedule for this position will be Monday - Thursday (30 hours) with a need to work hybrid between Altona and Port Melbourne locations.

Responsibilities

Represent Pinkerton’s core values of integrity, vigilance, and excellence.

Manage the Online Enforcement Program using the Corsearch AI-enabled e-commerce monitoring system to analyze and address trademark infringements.

Monitor online platforms for counterfeit products and coordinate takedown requests.

Oversee investigations into potential IP violations, manage test buy programs to gather evidence of counterfeiting, and collaborate with Pinkerton's Global Investigations Unit for supply chain probes and enforcement actions.

Assist IP lawyers with file management, document organization, and preparation of legal materials related to IP enforcement and litigation.

Process invoices and provide budget and forecasting.

Create reports including summaries of enforcement activities and risk assessments.

Provide training, communication and compliance support for suppliers and external stakeholders.

Access specialized units for integrated brand protection, including open-source intelligence (OSINT) research, persistent threat monitoring, and collaboration with law enforcement to eliminate counterfeit products.

All other duties, as assigned.

Qualifications
Bachelor’s degree in a relevant field such as intellectual property law, business administration, or a related discipline with at least five years of experience in brand protection, IP enforcement, or investigations.

Certified Fraud Examiner (CFE) or IP-specific credentials, preferred.

Knowledge of global IP regulations, trademark laws, and best practices in counterfeiting prevention.

Able to appropriately prioritize workload with competing deadlines.

Effective written and verbal communication skills.

Relationship management skills.

Able to collaborate with virtual teams to achieve common business goals.

Client orientated and results driven.

Able to maintain confidentiality and manage sensitive information.

Computer skills; Microsoft Office, Corsearch, AI-enabled analysis, and investigative software.
